prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response:
Even though I don't know you personally, I can suggest one thing that many people, including myself from time to time, tend to be wrong about: believing that we can multitask effectively.

The human brain is amazing, but evidence shows that it's simply not wired to perform more than one or two complex tasks at a time without a significant drop in performance for both tasks. When we try to do multiple things at once, we're actually just switching our attention very rapidly between them, leading toreduced focus, increased mistakes,and decreased productivity.

Instead, it's usually more efficient to focus on one task at a time, giving it your full attention,and then moving on to the next task once the first one is completed or reaching a natural stopping point. This approach, known as single-tasking or serial tasking, can help improve both the quality of your work and your overall productivity.
